mnemonics: SCOPE CRATER: A project SCOPE that's a CRATER (failure) often lacks these: S - Skilled Team, C - Clear Objectives, O - Open Communication, P - Proper Planning, E - Executive Support. C - Customer Involvement, R - Realistic Timeline, A - Adaptable Process, T - Technical Competence, E - Effective Tools, R - Risk Management
keyPoints: Success is multi-dimensional, often measured by being on time, on budget, meeting scope, and achieving customer satisfaction.
Common causes of failure include unclear requirements, poor communication, unrealistic schedules, and inadequate risk management.
The 'Triple Constraint' (Time, Cost, Scope) is a key framework; a change in one impacts the others.
commonMistakes: Focusing solely on the technical solution while neglecting stakeholder needs and business objectives.
Equating 'on time and on budget' with complete success, ignoring user adoption and delivered value.
Failing to proactively identify and manage project risks until they become critical issues.
